violations.claim: VIOLATION: A second call to approveOrder on an already-approved order
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P4 — temporal analysis
; LINE: 29
; VIOLATION: A second call to approveOrder on an already-approved order
;   overwrites approvedBy and re-fires the log — no idempotency guard exists.

(declare-const state_before_call1 Int)
(declare-const state_after_call1  Int)
(declare-const state_before_call2 Int)
(declare-const state_after_call2  Int)
(declare-const approved_by_call1  Int) ; 1 = set to non-null approver
(declare-const approved_by_call2  Int) ; 1 = overwritten by second approver

; First call: valid — source is submitted (1)
(assert (= state_before_call1 1))
; Code transition (call 1): state := 2, approvedBy := approver_1
(assert (= state_after_call1 2))
(assert (= approved_by_call1 1))

; Second call: state_before_call2 is the result of call 1
(assert (= state_before_call2 state_after_call1))
; VALID_TRANSITIONS["approved"] = ["shipped", "cancelled"] — "approved" NOT in list
; No guard prevents the second call: approveOrder checks nothing
; Code transition (call 2): state := 2, approvedBy overwritten
(assert (= state_after_call2 2))
(assert (= approved_by_call2 1))

; Violation: second call executed on already-approved order (state_before_call2 = 2)
(assert (= state_before_call2 2))
; Original approver record is silently overwritten
(assert (= approved_by_call1 1))
(assert (= approved_by_call2 1))

(check-sat)

clause_history.clause:

(assert (= state_before_call2 2))
; Original approver record is silently overwritten
(assert (= approved_by_call1 1))
(assert (= approved_by_call2 1))

(check-sat)
